Now I understand the existing test patterns. I need to:
1. Add a test to `tests/commands/one_shot/test_one_shot_dispatch.py` to verify that calling `dispatch_one_shot` with `ref="custom-ref"` sets `triggered_workflows[0][2] == "custom-ref"`
2. Add a test to `tests/commands/launch/test_launch_cmd.py` to verify that `erk launch pr-address --pr 123 --ref custom-branch` captures the ref
3. Add a test to `tests/commands/pr/test_dispatch.py` to verify that `erk pr dispatch 42 --ref custom-branch` captures the ref

Dispatch should: 25→ - Validate the PR has the erk-plan label and is OPEN 26→ - Sync local branch ref to remote and commit impl-context via git plumbing 27→ - Trigger workflow with plan_backend="planned_pr" in inputs 28→ - NOT create a new branch or PR 29→ """ 30→ runner = CliRunner()

Now I have a clear picture of the test patterns. I need to add:
1. A test in `test_one_shot_dispatch.py` that calls `dispatch_one_shot` with explicit `ref="custom-ref"` and asserts `triggered_workflows[0][2] == "custom-ref"`
2. A test in `test_launch_cmd.py` that calls `erk launch pr-address --pr 123 --ref custom-branch` and asserts the ref is captured
3. A test in `test_dispatch.py` that calls `erk pr dispatch 42 --ref custom-branch` and asserts the ref is captured
I need to check the existing test calls to `dispatch_one_shot` in the test file to see if they're already passing the `ref` parameter or if I need to update them to include `ref=None` for backward compatibility. I need to fix those test calls too—there are four places in the test file where `dispatch_one_shot` is invoked without the new `ref` parameter, so I'll update each of them to pass `ref=None` and add a test case for when `ref` is explicitly set to a custom value.

1→--- 2→title: dispatch_ref Configuration 3→read_when: 4→ - "configuring which branch workflow_dispatch targets" 5→ - "working with .erk/config.toml dispatch settings" 6→ - "debugging workflow dispatch targeting the wrong branch" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "assuming dispatch_ref is project-level config" 9→ warning: "dispatch_ref is repo-level config (.erk/config.toml), overridable at local level (.erk/config.local.toml). It is not project-level." 10→--- 11→ 12→# dispatch_ref Configuration 13→ 14→Override the default branch used for GitHub Actions `workflow_dispatch` events. By default, erk dispatches workflows against the repository's default branch (usually `master`). The `dispatch_ref` config allows targeting a different branch. 15→ 16→## Configuration 17→ 18→Top-level key in `.erk/config.toml`: 19→ 20→```toml 21→dispatch_ref = "my-custom-branch" 22→``` 23→ 24→Can be overridden per-user in `.erk/config.local.toml`: 25→ 26→```toml 27→dispatch_ref = "my-dev-branch" 28→``` 29→ 30→## Type Definition
